claim: Autologous transplantation of mesenchymal stem cells causes fewer opportunistic infections than induction therapy with anti-interleukin-2 receptor antibodies.

result.claim: Autologous transplantation of mesenchymal stem cells causes fewer opportunistic infections than induction therapy with anti-interleukin-2 receptor antibodies.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from a randomized controlled trial (Paper ID: 10582939). The study, which compared autologous mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) therapy with anti-IL-2 receptor antibody induction therapy in living-related kidney transplant patients, found a significantly decreased risk of opportunistic infections in the MSC-treated groups. Specifically, the abstract states: "during the 1-year follow-up, combined analysis of MSC-treated groups revealed significantly decreased risk of opportunistic infections than the control group (hazard ratio, 0.42; 95% CI, 0.20-0.85, P=.02)". This direct comparison and statistically significant finding from a high-quality study design provide strong support for the claim.

evidence.text: Autospecific CD4(+) T cells showed increased Th2 cytokine responses in the absence of IL-12.
evidence.source: Dual role of the IL-12/IFN-gamma axis in the development of autoimmune myocarditis: induction by IL-12 and protection by IFN-gamma.

evidence.text: Autophagy is a recently recognized immune effector mechanism against intracellular pathogens.

evidence.text: Delayed T cell recovery and restricted T cell receptor diversity after allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation are associated with increased risks of infection and cancer relapse.

evidence.text: Fifty-one patients in the control group received anti-IL-2 receptor antibody plus standard-dose CNIs.
evidence.source: Induction therapy with autologous mesenchymal stem cells in living-related kidney transplants: a randomized controlled trial.

evidence.text: The presence of an autoimmune disease-associated IL2RA haplotype is associated with a reduction in the ability of Tregs to suppress proliferation of autologous effector T cells.

evidence.text: Patients were inoculated with marrow-derived autologous MSC (1-2 x 10(6)/kg) at kidney reperfusion and two weeks later.
evidence.source: Induction therapy with autologous mesenchymal stem cells in living-related kidney transplants: a randomized controlled trial.

evidence.text: Human embryonic-stem-cell-derived embryoid bodies generate a KDRlow/C-KIT(CD117)neg population after induction with activin A, bone morphogenetic protein 4 (BMP4), basic fibroblast growth factor (bFGF, also known as FGF2), vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF, also known as VEGFA) and dickkopf homolog 1 (DKK1) in serum-free media.

evidence.text: After 6 months, 4 of 53 patients (7.5%) in the autologous MSC plus standard-dose CNI group had biopsy-confirmed acute rejection.
evidence.source: Induction therapy with autologous mesenchymal stem cells in living-related kidney transplants: a randomized controlled trial.
